rationale: The obligation requires proving det_clone_up_to_view_equal(r1, r2) := (r1.id == r2.id) for two EndPoint values whose only known property is r1@ == self_@ and r2@ == self_@. Those view-equalities give r1.id@ == r2.id@ (Seq-level), but Verus's spec equality `==` on `Vec<u8>` is opaque: no axiom in vstd::std_specs::vec maps Seq view-equality to structural Vec equality. Empirically confirmed by a minimal Verus test (asserting `v1 == v2` or even `v1 =~= v2` from `v1@ == v2@` for `Vec<u8>` fails). The external_body clone_up_to_view only guarantees view equality, so structural Vec equality of the cloned ids is not derivable. The obligation is unprovable without relaxing the equal-fn to compare via @ or view_equal.
